name: typed-gdn2-mixture-winner-8M
form: typed-gdn2 (TypedHeadMixtureLayer): horizontal population of NATIVE recurrent head types in one layer -- native FLA GatedDeltaNet-2 recall heads (allow_neg_eigval=True, N=32) + frozen E98 corner specialists (E97-track/count/latch/nonlin via fixed_pop), summed into the residual stream
objective: fitness = 0.5*mean + 0.5*min over 6 probes {mqar_recall,s5,anbncn,flag_hold,iter_nonlin,mixed}; length-extrap T in {128,256,512,1024}
verdict: GDN-2-heavy mixture WINS on mean (0.919) and worst-case floor (0.807). Recovers GDN-like MQAR recall (0.807) that E98 cannot (0.423); preserves S5/count/latch/nonlin. GDN-2 (neg-eigval + gated decay) subsumes track/count/latch -> CMA sets them to zero. The all-GDN ablation (no nonlin heads) MATCHES the mixture (mean 0.915, nonlin 0.953) -> the nonlinear specialist is NOT justified at 8M; the heterogeneity that pays off is GDN-2's own internal generality, not added specialist head types. Next-scale candidate = a DENSE GDN-2 BACKBONE (allow_neg_eigval + gated decay), not E98, not plain DeltaNet, with specialist fraction an open question for the LM pilot.
provenance: typed-gdn-2-head CMA STAGE; LHS14->CMA pop8, 8 gens (conv gen6), fp32 schedule-free; validated 6 probes x 2 seeds x 4000 steps vs cma-capability E98 winner + param-matched 8M native DeltaNet reference
